warning: AI-assisted monitoring. Verify against official legislation before product approval, export, label declaration, or customer compliance confirmation.

rules.notes: The Codex General Standard for Food Additives (GSFA, Codex STAN 192-1995) sets forth conditions for permitted food additives. Specific provisions are available in the searchable online database.
rules.evidenceQuote: The "Codex General Standard for Food Additives" (GSFA, Codex STAN 192-1995) sets forth the conditions under which permitted food additives may be used in all foods... This database provides, in a searchable format, all the provisions for food additives that have been adopted by the Codex Alimentarius Commission.

rules.parameter: Maximum levels for contaminants and naturally occurring toxicants
rules.notes: Codex establishes maximum levels (MLs) and guideline levels for contaminants and naturally occurring toxicants in food. These are detailed in the General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CXS 193-1995) and other related texts.
rules.evidenceQuote: The Codex maximum level (ML) for a contaminant in a food or feed commodity is the maximum concentration of that substance recommended by the Codex Alimentarius Commission to be legally permitted in that commodity. The Codex Committee on Contaminants in Food (CCCF) establishes and endorses permitted maximum levels or guideline levels for contaminants and naturally occurring toxicants in food and feed. Related Codex Texts Reference Title CXS 193-1995 General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed.

rules.parameter: Food additives and their conditions of use
rules.limit: Verification required against the Union list of food additives in Annex II of Regulation (EC) No 1333/2008
rules.notes: The database serves as a tool to inform about approved food additives and their conditions of use in the EU.
rules.evidenceQuote: This database can serve as a tool to inform about the food additives approved for use in food in the EU and their conditions of use. It is based on the Union list of food additives. This list is in Annex II of Regulation (EC) No 1333/2008.

rules.parameter: Food safety criteria for relevant foodborne bacteria, their toxins and metabolites (e.g., Salmonella, Listeria monocytogenes, Enterobacter sakazakii, staphylococcal enterotoxins, histamine) and process hygiene criteria
rules.limit: Compliance with Commission Regulation (EC) No 2073/2005 required
rules.sourceTitle: Microbiological criteria - Food Safety - European Commission
rules.notes: This regulation defines the acceptability of a product or a batch of food and lays down process hygiene criteria to indicate the correct functioning of the production process.
rules.evidenceQuote: Commission Regulation (EC) No 2073/2005 on microbiological criteria for foods, applicable from 1 January 2006, lays down food safety criteria for relevant foodborne bacteria, their toxins and metabolites, such as Salmonella , Listeria monocytogenes , Enterobacter sakazakii , staphylococcal enterotoxins and histamine in specific foods. These criteria define the acceptability of a product or a batch of food applicable to products placed on the market. In addition, this Regulation lays down certain process hygiene criteria to indicate the correct functioning of the production process.

rules.parameter: Data label requirements for packaged products
rules.sourceTitle: GSO OIML R79:2021 OIML R79:2015 مواصفة قياسية خليجية --> متطلبات بطاقة بيانات المنتجات المعبأة
rules.notes: This standard outlines general requirements for data labels on packaged products, applicable to seasoning, snack food, and processed foods.
rules.evidenceQuote: متطلبات بطاقة بيانات المنتجات المعبأة

rules.sourceTitle: GSO 9:2022 لائحة فنية خليجية --> بطاقات المواد الغذائية المعبأة
rules.notes: This regulation outlines general labelling requirements for all prepackaged foodstuffs, which would include allergen declarations, ingredient lists, and potentially warnings relevant to seasoning, snack food, and processed foods.
rules.evidenceQuote: بطاقات المواد الغذائية المعبأة

rules.sourceTitle: GSO 150-1:2013 لائحة فنية خليجية --> فترات صلاحية المنتجات الغذائية - الجزء الأول : فترات الصلاحية الإلزامية
rules.notes: This regulation specifies mandatory shelf life periods for food products, which must be declared on the label for seasoning, snack food, and processed foods.
rules.evidenceQuote: فترات الصلاحية الإلزامية

rules.sourceTitle: GSO 150-2:2013 مواصفة قياسية خليجية --> فترات صلاحية المنتجات الغذائية - الجزء الثاني : فترات الصلاحية الاختيارية
rules.notes: This regulation provides guidelines for optional shelf life periods for food products, which may be declared on the label for seasoning, snack food, and processed foods.
rules.evidenceQuote: فترات الصلاحية الاختيارية

rules.sourceTitle: GSO OIML R87:2021 OIML R87:2016 مواصفة قياسية خليجية --> كمية المنتج في العبوات المعبأة مسبقاً
rules.notes: This standard relates to the accurate declaration of product quantity on labels for prepackaged products, including seasoning, snack food, and processed foods.
rules.evidenceQuote: كمية المنتج في العبوات المعبأة مسبقاً

rules.sourceTitle: GSO 9:2022 Labeling of prepackaged food stuffs
rules.notes: This regulation sets out the general requirements for labelling of prepackaged food.
rules.evidenceQuote: GSO 9:2022 لائحة فنية خليجية --> بطاقات المواد الغذائية المعبأة

rules.sourceTitle: GSO 2055-1:2015 HALAL FOOD - Part 1 : General Requirements
rules.notes: This regulation outlines the general requirements for Halal food.
rules.evidenceQuote: GSO 2055-1:2015 لائحة فنية خليجية --> الأغذية الحلال – الجزء الأول : الاشتراطات العامة للأغذية الحلال

rules.sourceTitle: GSO 150-1:2013 Expiration dates for food products -Part 1 : Mandatory expiration dates
rules.notes: This regulation specifies mandatory expiration dates for food products.
rules.evidenceQuote: GSO 150-1:2013 لائحة فنية خليجية --> فترات صلاحية المنتجات الغذائية - الجزء الأول : فترات الصلاحية الإلزامية

rules.sourceTitle: GSO 150-2:2013 Expiration dates for food products - Part 2 : Voluntary expiration dates
rules.notes: This standard provides guidelines for voluntary expiration dates for food products.
rules.evidenceQuote: GSO 150-2:2013 مواصفة قياسية خليجية --> فترات صلاحية المنتجات الغذائية - الجزء الثاني : فترات الصلاحية الاختيارية

rules.foodCategory: Foodstuffs intended for human consumption, food ingredients
rules.notes: This technical regulation specifies microbiological limits for various foodstuffs and food ingredients.
rules.evidenceQuote: This GSO technical regulation is concerned with microbiological limits for some foodstuffs intended for human consumption and for some food ingredients used in food industry.

rules.parameter: Permitted food additives and their maximum limits
rules.notes: Specific permitted food additives and their maximum limits for various food categories, including seasoning and snack products, are detailed in the referenced compendium. Manufacturers must consult the full document for compliance.
rules.evidenceQuote: Compendium of Food Additives Regulations

rules.notes: Specific requirements for allergen declaration on food labels for all food products, including seasoning and snack products, are detailed in the referenced compendium. Manufacturers must consult the full document for compliance.
rules.evidenceQuote: Compendium of Labelling & Display Regulations

rules.notes: General labelling and display requirements for food products, including seasoning and snack products, are detailed in the referenced compendium. This includes aspects like nutritional information, ingredient list, net quantity, etc. Manufacturers must consult the full document for compliance.
rules.evidenceQuote: Compendium of Labelling & Display Regulations

rules.parameter: Declaration of salt/sodium content and related claims
rules.notes: Requirements for the declaration of salt/sodium content on food labels for products, including seasoning and snack products, and conditions for making claims related to salt/sodium content, are detailed in the referenced compendium. Manufacturers must consult the full document for compliance.
rules.evidenceQuote: Compendium of Labelling & Display Regulations

rules.sourceTitle: Surat Edaran Nomor 2 Tahun 2026 tentang Ketentuan Produksi, Importasi, Registrasi, dan Peredaran Bahan Tambahan Pangan Dinitrogen Monoksida (N2O)
rules.notes: This circular letter specifies provisions for the production, import, registration, and circulation of Dinitrogen Monoxide (N2O) as a food additive. Actual limits and conditions must be verified from the full document.
rules.evidenceQuote: SE Nomor 2 Tahun 2026 Surat Edaran Nomor 2 Tahun 2026 tentang Ketentuan Produksi, Importasi, Registrasi, dan Peredaran Bahan Tambahan Pangan Dinitrogen Monoksida (N2O)

rules.sourceTitle: PERKABPOM Nomor 34 Tahun 2019 Tentang Kategori Pangan
rules.notes: This regulation defines food categories, which is essential for understanding the scope of other food regulations, proper product classification, and related labelling requirements. The full document must be consulted.
rules.evidenceQuote: PERKABPOM Nomor 34 Tahun 2019 Tentang Kategori Pangan

rules.sourceTitle: Jaringan Dokumentasi dan Informasi Hukum Badan Pengawas Obat dan Makanan RI
rules.notes: Specific regulations for processed foods, including seasoning and snack products, must be consulted in the BPOM legal database. There are 281 regulations listed under 'Pangan Olahan'.
rules.evidenceQuote: Pangan Olahan 281 Peraturan

rules.notes: Additives (excluding natural flavoring agents and generally consumed substances used as additives) and food containing them are prohibited for sale, production, import, etc., unless the Minister of Health, Labour and Welfare specifies them as unlikely to cause harm to human health.
rules.evidenceQuote: Additives (excluding natural flavoring agents and things that have generally been served for human consumption, and are used as additives) and preparations and food containing additives must not be sold... except for cases that the Minister of Health, Labour and Welfare specifies as unlikely to cause harm to human health

rules.parameter: Contamination, foreign substances, or other grounds causing harm
rules.notes: General prohibition for food likely to cause harm to human health.
rules.evidenceQuote: those which are likely to cause harm to human health due to contamination or the addition of foreign substances or on other grounds.

rules.foodCategory: Meat, bones, milk, organs, blood of livestock/poultry
rules.notes: Prohibition of selling meat, bones, milk, organs, and blood from livestock or poultry that have or are suspected to have specified diseases or abnormalities, or have died, except for cases specified by Ministry of Health, Labour and Welfare Order.
rules.evidenceQuote: The meat, bones, milk, organs, and blood of livestock... which have or are suspected to have any of the diseases listed in item (i) or item (iii), have any of the abnormalities listed in item (i) or item (iii), or have died... must not be sold as food

rules.parameter: Residues of agricultural chemicals, feed additives, veterinary drugs
rules.limit: Must not exceed MHLW-specified safe quantities
rules.notes: Food containing residues of agricultural chemicals, feed additives, or pharmaceutical products for animals in quantities exceeding MHLW-specified safe limits is prohibited. Verification of specific MHLW limits is required.
rules.evidenceQuote: Food in which substances that are the ingredients of agricultural chemicals... feed... and pharmaceutical products... remain in a quantity exceeding the quantity that the Minister of Health, Labour and Welfare specifies as unlikely to cause harm to human health... must not be produced, imported, processed, used, cooked, preserved, for the purpose of sale, or sold

rules.limit: Not allowed (unless specified safe by MHLW)
rules.notes: Prohibition of food or additives containing or suspected to contain toxic or harmful substances, unless the Minister of Health, Labour and Welfare specifies them as unlikely to cause harm.
rules.evidenceQuote: those which contain or bear toxic or harmful substances or are suspected to contain or bear such substances; provided, however, that this does not apply when the Minister of Health, Labour and Welfare specifies that the substances are unlikely to cause harm to human health

rules.foodCategory: Imported livestock/poultry meat, milk, organs, and products thereof
rules.notes: Imported livestock/poultry meat, milk, organs, and products thereof must have certificates from the exporting country's governmental organization, confirming they are free from specified diseases/abnormalities.
rules.evidenceQuote: must not be imported as food for the purpose of sale, unless they have certificates or their copies attached which have been issued by a governmental organization of an exporting country, and which certify that they are not the meat, milk or organs of livestock, or, the meat or organs of poultry, or, products thereof which have or are suspected to have any of the diseases listed in each item of the preceding paragraph, have any of the abnormalities listed in each item of the same paragraph, or have died

rules.foodCategory: Food and additives requiring production site sanitation management certification
rules.notes: Food and additives specified by MHLW as requiring certification of production site sanitation management must not be imported for sale unless accompanied by certificates from the exporting country's governmental organization.
rules.evidenceQuote: The food and additives specified by Ministry of Health, Labour and Welfare Order as requiring certification of the conditions of food sanitation management at the production site... must not be imported for the purpose of sale, unless they have certificates or copies thereof attached which have been issued by a governmental organization of an exporting country and which contain the relevant matters.

rules.parameter: Notification of harm from designated ingredients
rules.notes: Businesses handling food with MHLW-designated ingredients must notify the relevant prefectural governor, etc. without delay if the food causes or may cause harm to human health.
rules.evidenceQuote: A business that handles food containing any ingredient or material which requires special attention... must notify the relevant prefectural governor, etc. of that information without delay pursuant to Ministry of Health, Labour and Welfare Order.

rules.parameter: Production, processing, use, cooking, preservation methods, and ingredients
rules.notes: Food or additives must conform to criteria established by the Minister of Health, Labour and Welfare for methods of production, processing, use, cooking, or preservation, and to standards for ingredients. Verification of specific MHLW criteria and standards is required.
rules.evidenceQuote: the Minister of Health, Labour and Welfare may establish the criteria for the methods of producing, processing, using, cooking, or preserving food or additives... or may establish standards for the ingredients of food or additives... food or additives must not be produced, processed, used, cooked, or preserved using methods that do not conform to such criteria; food or additives that do not conform to such criteria must not be sold or imported; and food or additives that do not conform to such standards must not be produced, imported, processed, used, cooked, preserved, or sold.

rules.foodCategory: Food and additives requiring important process control measures
rules.notes: Food and additives specified by MHLW as requiring important process control measures must only be imported if produced or processed in MHLW-specified countries, regions, or facilities that implement those measures.
rules.evidenceQuote: The food and additives specified by Ministry of Health, Labour and Welfare Order as requiring measures to control particularly important processes... must not be imported for the purpose of sale, unless they have been produced or processed in the countries or regions, or facilities specified by the Minister of Health, Labour and Welfare as actually taking those measures.

rules.limit: Not allowed if likely to cause harm
rules.notes: Prohibition of food contaminated with pathogens or suspected to be so, if likely to cause harm to human health.
rules.evidenceQuote: those which are contaminated with pathogens or are suspected to be so, and which are likely to cause harm to human health

rules.parameter: Class II Chemical Preservatives (e.g., sorbic acid, benzoic acid)
rules.limit: Permitted levels must be calculated using SFA's Calculation Tool if two or more are used
rules.notes: This applies when two or more Class II chemical preservatives are used in the food.
rules.evidenceQuote: If two or more Class II chemical preservatives are to be used in the food (e.g. sorbic acid or benzoic acid), use the Calculation Tool for Class II Chemical Preservatives to calculate the permitted levels that should be used.

rules.limit: Must be assessed for safety by SFA
rules.notes: Applies to all food sold in Singapore.
rules.evidenceQuote: Food additives must be assessed for safety by SFA before they can be used in food sold in Singapore.

rules.notes: Verification required against SFA's List of Permitted Food Additives or Food Additives Search tool.
rules.evidenceQuote: Only food additives that have been assessed by SFA are permitted for use in food products sold in Singapore.

rules.notes: Limits depend on the additive and food type. Refer to 'Guidance Information on Requirements for Use of Food Additives'.
rules.evidenceQuote: Different limits apply, depending on the additive and the type of food products. Use the Guidance Information on Requirements for Use of Food Additives to learn more about the different types of additives and the corresponding regulations that impose the limits.

rules.notes: An application can be submitted to SFA for evaluation of non-permitted additives.
rules.evidenceQuote: If your food product contains an additive that is not permitted, do not import or sell it.

rules.limit: Must be within SFA’s regulatory limits
rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Contaminants in Food
rules.notes: All food businesses must comply.
rules.evidenceQuote: Contaminants (also known as incidental constituents) in food sold in Singapore must be within SFA’s regulatory limits.

rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Contaminants in Food
rules.evidenceQuote: If your food or food products contain contaminants or incidental constituents that exceed the regulatory limits, do not import or sell them.

rules.foodCategory: Meat and Food Derived from Meat
rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Contaminants in Food
rules.notes: Prohibition under Regulation 33 of the Food Regulations.
rules.evidenceQuote: Regulation 33 of the Food Regulations prohibits the import or sale of meat and any food derived from meat which contains residues of the following compounds: diethylstibestrol (3, 4-bis(p-hydroxyphenyl)-3-hexene) hexoestrol (3, 4-bis(p-hydroxyphenyl)-n-hexane) dienoestrol (3, 4-bis(p-hydroxyphenyl)-2, 4-hexadiene)

rules.limit: Compliance required with Singapore's regulatory limits or Codex MRLs
rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Contaminants in Food
rules.notes: Codex MRLs apply if no regulatory limits are specified in the Food Regulations.
rules.evidenceQuote: Pesticide residues Singapore's regulatory limits Codex MRLs (if there are no regulatory limits specified in the Food Regulations)

rules.parameter: Various Contaminants (e.g., heavy metals, mycotoxins, veterinary drug residues, pesticide residues)
rules.limit: Verification required against SFA's specific documents
rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Contaminants in Food
rules.notes: Refer to SFA's documents for Microbiological standards, Heavy metals, Mycotoxins and Marine biotoxins, Veterinary drug residues, Pesticide residues, and Other incidental constituents.
rules.evidenceQuote: There are various limits imposed on different types of contaminants or incidental constituents in food. For more details, please refer to the relevant documents below: Microbiological standards, Heavy metals, Mycotoxins and Marine biotoxins, Veterinary drug residues, Pesticide residues, Other incidental constituents.

rules.limit: Compliance required with SFA's established MRLs
rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Contaminants in Food
rules.notes: MRLs can be downloaded from the SFA website and will be included in future amendments of the Food Regulations.
rules.evidenceQuote: SFA has also assessed the risk of the presence of other veterinary drug residues in various food commodities, and has established a list of maximum residue limits (MRLs) for veterinary drug residues for compliance by food businesses with effect from 1 May 2020.

rules.limit: Must be declared in the list of ingredients on food labels
rules.notes: Applies to prepacked foods for sale in Singapore.
rules.evidenceQuote: All ingredients and additives used in food products must be declared in list of ingredients on food labels.

rules.limit: Verification required against SFA's microbiological standards
rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Contaminants in Food
rules.notes: Separate standards for ready-to-eat food and non-ready-to-eat food, with updated standards for non-ready-to-eat food from 4 Mar 2024.
rules.evidenceQuote: Microbiological standards Ready-to-eat food Non-ready-to-eat food (till 3 Mar 2024) Non-ready-to-eat food (from 4 Mar 2024)

rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Contaminants in Food
rules.evidenceQuote: Ready-to-eat (RTE) food are not required to be tested for total plate count (TPC).

rules.parameter: γ-Aminobutyric Acid (GABA) produced by microbial fermentation
rules.sourceTitle: Use Restrictions and Labeling Requirements of γ-Aminobutyric Acid Produced by Microbial Fermentation as a Food Ingredient
rules.notes: Specific use restrictions and labeling requirements are detailed in the referenced regulation.
rules.evidenceQuote: Use Restrictions and Labeling Requirements of γ-Aminobutyric Acid Produced by Microbial Fermentation as a Food Ingredient

rules.foodCategory: Products containing these allergens as ingredient or food additive
rules.parameter: Cephalopods and cephalopod products (cuttlefish, neritic squid, octopus, squid, takoyaki, dried shredded squid); Spiral shells and Spiral shell products (escargot, mussel, clam, oyster, scallop, mytilus, meretrix lusoria, abalone, scallop sauce, scallop candy, spiral shell sauce); Seeds and seed products (Sunflower seed, melon seed - excluding highly refined/purified sunflower seed oil); Kiwis and kiwi products (Kiwi jam, dried kiwi)
rules.limit: Presence as an ingredient or food additive
rules.sourceTitle: Regulations Governing Food Allergen Labeling on the Recommended Labeling Allergens
rules.notes: Labeling is recommended for these allergens.
rules.evidenceQuote: food allergen labeling is also recommended for the products that contain any of the following allergens as an ingredient or food additive

rules.parameter: Crustacean shellfish, mango, peanuts, sesame seeds, milk and goat milk, eggs, tree nuts, gluten-containing cereals, soybeans, fish
rules.limit: Presence as an ingredient or food additive
rules.sourceTitle: Regulations Governing Food Allergen Labeling on the Recommended Labeling Allergens
rules.notes: Labeling is required if these are present as ingredients or food additives.
rules.evidenceQuote: food allergen labeling is required for the products that contain any of the following 11 allergens as an ingredient or food additive

rules.sourceTitle: Regulations Governing Food Allergen Labeling on the Recommended Labeling Allergens
rules.notes: Labeling is required if the residue sulphur dioxide is 10 mg/kg or more.
rules.evidenceQuote: sulphite as the residue sulphur dioxide for 10 or more milligrams per kilogram of food

rules.sourceTitle: Standards for Pesticide Residue Limits in Foods
rules.notes: Specific limits are detailed in the referenced standard.
rules.evidenceQuote: Standards for Pesticide Residue Limits in Foods

rules.sourceTitle: Revised Regulations on Nutrition Claim for Prepackaged Food Products
rules.notes: Specific regulations for nutrition claims are detailed in the referenced document.
rules.evidenceQuote: Revised Regulations on Nutrition Claim for Prepackaged Food Products

rules.foodCategory: Food products with risk of allergen cross-contamination
rules.sourceTitle: Regulations Governing Food Allergen Labeling on the Recommended Labeling Allergens
rules.notes: Suggested statement: 'The plant, equipment or production line used to produce the product is also used to process○○” or synonymous terms.
rules.evidenceQuote: If there is a risk of a food product being affected by allergen cross-contamination... the following statement of precautionary allergen labeling is suggested: “ The plant, equipment or production line used to produce the product is also used to process○○” or the synonymous terms.

rules.notes: Food additives must comply with specific regulations and lists of permitted/non-permitted ingredients.
rules.evidenceQuote: วัตถุเจือปนอาหาร, ส่วนประกอบที่อนุญาต/ไม่อนุญาตให้ใช้ในอาหาร

rules.notes: Food products must comply with established standards for contaminants.
rules.evidenceQuote: มาตรฐานอาหารที่มีสารปนเปื้อน

rules.notes: Maximum Residue Limits (MRLs) for contaminants must comply with the ASEAN MRLs list.
rules.evidenceQuote: บัญชีค่าปริมาณสารพิษตกค้างสูงสุดของอาเซียน (ASEAN MRLs) (ฉบับปรับปรุง กุมภาพันธ์ 2569)

rules.notes: Food products must comply with established standards for toxins and veterinary drug residues.
rules.evidenceQuote: มาตรฐานอาหารที่มีสารพิษ-ยาสัตว์ตกค้าง

rules.notes: Food products must comply with the requirements for food and nutrition labeling.
rules.evidenceQuote: การแสดงฉลากอาหารและฉลากโภชนาการ

rules.notes: Certain food products are prohibited from production, import, or sale in Thailand.
rules.evidenceQuote: อาหารที่ห้ามผลิต นำเข้า หรือจำหน่าย

rules.notes: Food products must comply with established standards for pathogenic microorganisms.
rules.evidenceQuote: มาตรฐานอาหารด้านจุลินทรีย์ที่ทำให้เกิดโรค

rules.notes: Food additives and color additives must be listed in FDA regulations (21 CFR Parts 172, 173 and Parts 73, 74 respectively) or evaluated by FEMA/JECFA for flavoring substances.
rules.evidenceQuote: Food additives and color additives that are listed in FDA regulations (21 CFR Parts 172, 173 and Parts 73, 74 respectively), and flavoring substances evaluated by FEMA* and JECFA*.

rules.notes: GRAS substances must be listed in FDA regulations (21 CFR Parts 182 and 184) or in the inventory of GRAS Notices.
rules.evidenceQuote: Generally Recognized as Safe ("GRAS") substances that are listed in FDA regulations (21 CFR Parts 182 and 184).

rules.notes: Substances approved for specific uses in foods prior to September 6, 1958, must be listed in 21 CFR Part 181.
rules.evidenceQuote: Substances approved for specific uses in foods prior to September 6, 1958, known as prior-sanctioned substances (21 CFR Part 181).

rules.parameter: Prohibited Substances and Delisted Color Additives
rules.notes: Prohibited substances are listed in FDA regulations (21 CFR Part 189) and delisted color additives in 21 CFR 81.10 and/or 81.30.
rules.evidenceQuote: Prohibited substances that are listed in FDA regulations (21 CFR Part 189) as prohibited from use in food (labeled as "PROHIBITED" or "PROHIBITED WITH EXCEPTIONS"). Delisted color additives in FDA regulations (21 CFR 81.10 and/or 81.30) (labeled as "DELISTED").

rules.notes: The nine major food allergens are milk, eggs, fish, Crustacean shellfish, tree nuts, peanuts, wheat, soybeans, and sesame. All FDA requirements applicable to major food allergens, including labeling and manufacturing requirements, apply to sesame as of January 1, 2023.
rules.evidenceQuote: The major food allergens are milk, eggs, fish, Crustacean shellfish, tree nuts, peanuts, wheat, soybeans, and sesame. All FDA requirements applicable to major food allergens, including labeling and manufacturing requirements, apply to sesame as of January 1, 2023.

rules.notes: Guidance Level for 3-MCPD in acid-hydrolyzed protein and Asian-style sauces.
rules.evidenceQuote: 3-Monochloropropane-1,2-Diol (3-MCPD) Acid-hydrolyzed protein and Asian-style sauces Guidance Level 1 ppm CPG 500.500

rules.notes: Action Level for total aflatoxins in foods, peanuts and peanut products, pistachio nuts, and Brazil nuts.
rules.evidenceQuote: Aflatoxins, total Foods Action Level 20 ppb CPG 555.400; Aflatoxins, total Peanuts and peanut products Action Level 20 ppb CPG 570.375; Aflatoxins, total Pistachio nuts Action Level 20 ppb CPG 570.500; Aflatoxins, total Brazil nuts Action Level 20 ppb CPG 570.200

rules.parameter: Chemical Contaminants (Tolerances, Action Levels, Guidance Levels)
rules.notes: Contaminant levels for poisonous or deleterious substances are published in various guidance documents and regulations (21 CFR parts 109 and 509). The Chemical Contaminants Transparency Tool provides a consolidated list.
rules.evidenceQuote: The FDA has published a variety of contaminant levels for poisonous or deleterious substances in human food in various guidance documents and regulations for industry. For ease of reference, the Chemical Contaminants Transparency Tool provides a consolidated list of these levels. Tolerances and action levels are defined in 21 Code of Federal Regulations (CFR) parts 109 and 509.

rules.notes: Advisory statements are not required by law and should only be used to address unavoidable cross-contact after good manufacturing practices have been incorporated. They must be truthful and not misleading.
rules.evidenceQuote: Such statements are not required by law and can be used to address unavoidable “cross-contact,” only if manufacturers have incorporated good manufacturing processes in their facility and have taken every precaution to avoid cross-contact [...] advisory statements should not be used as a substitute for adhering to current good manufacturing practices and must be truthful and not misleading.

rules.notes: The CGMP & PC rule (21 CFR part 117) establishes requirements for allergen preventive controls to prevent allergen cross-contact in manufacturing and packaging and to prevent undeclared allergens. Facilities must put written procedures in place.
rules.evidenceQuote: The FDA’s “Current Good Manufacturing Practice, Hazard Analysis, and Risk-Based Preventive Controls for Human Food” rule (CGMP & PC rule, 21 CFR part 117) establishes requirements applicable to establishments that manufacture, process, pack, or hold human food. The CGMP & PC rule includes requirements for allergen preventive controls to prevent allergen cross-contact in manufacturing and packaging and to prevent undeclared allergens.

rules.notes: Color additives made from cochineal extract and carmine must be declared on the label of all food and cosmetic products.
rules.evidenceQuote: Color additives made from cochineal extract and carmine, which are derived from insects, have been identified as allergenic substances that must be declared on the label of all food and cosmetic products.

rules.notes: All products containing FD&C Yellow No. 5 must identify it on their labels.
rules.evidenceQuote: The FDA requires all products containing FD&C Yellow No. 5 to identify it on their labels so consumers who are sensitive to the dye can avoid it.

rules.notes: The FDA issued a final rule defining 'gluten-free' for food labeling. Compliance requirements for fermented and hydrolyzed foods bearing the 'gluten-free' claim were established on August 12, 2020.
rules.evidenceQuote: On August 2, 2013, the FDA issued a final rule defining “gluten-free” for food labeling [...] On August 12, 2020, the FDA issued a final rule to establish compliance requirements for fermented and hydrolyzed foods, or foods that contain fermented or hydrolyzed ingredients, bearing the “gluten-free” claim.

rules.notes: The food source of a major food allergen must appear in parentheses following the ingredient name (e.g., 'lecithin (soy)') OR immediately after or next to the list of ingredients in a 'contains' statement (e.g., 'Contains wheat, milk, and soy.'). The specific type of tree nut, species of fish, and Crustacean shellfish must be declared.
rules.evidenceQuote: The name of the food source of a major food allergen must appear: In parentheses following the name of the ingredient. [...] — OR — Immediately after or next to the list of ingredients in a “contains” statement. [...] The law also requires that the type of tree nut (for example, almonds, pecans, walnuts), and the species of fish (for example, bass, flounder, cod) and Crustacean shellfish (for example, crab, lobster, shrimp) to be declared.

rules.notes: Various sulfiting agents must be declared on food labels when present in food and the concentration is ≥10 parts per million total sulfites.
rules.evidenceQuote: Various sulfiting agents, including sodium bisulfite, are allowed as food ingredients. But due in part to adverse reactions to them, such as asthma in sensitive individuals, they must be declared on food labels when present in food and the concentration in the food is ≥10 parts per million total sulfites.
